entries.mem0ai/mem0: The official Hermes Agent memory provider, offering a universal layer for user, session, and agent state retention. It utilizes a token-efficient ADD-only extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval fusing semantic, BM25, and entity signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent featuring a self-wiring knowledge graph with automated entity extraction. It combines vector retrieval with graph-based backlink ranking using a local-first PGLite architecture.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system that implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent evolution. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper compatible with major providers and local models via Ollama.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful memory library that maintains persistent data for users, agents, and evolving entities. It enables natural language querying of user traits and interaction history via Python and TypeScript SDKs.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive context harness designed for complex tasks through iterative self-improvement and structured evaluation. Its five-role architecture generates reusable playbooks and datasets for local model distillation.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A Lossless Context Management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware context compaction. It ensures no message is lost by utilizing a SQLite-backed store for raw data preservation.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for Honcho that enables private, cross-session memory for Hermes Agent. It includes automated Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis setup scripts and supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP server and agent plugins using a hybrid of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that reduces prompt tokens by up to 36% through deduplication. It boosts prefill speeds by 1.5–3× via optimized prefix sharing for Hermes Agent and vLLM.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open YAML engram format for local storage. It employs ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system that indexes codebases and media using automatic summarization and OCR. It uses edge tags to create bidirectional relationships between notes, accessible via MCP and CLI.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system providing sub-millisecond latency through local SQLite storage. It features native vector and full-text search with automated episodic consolidation cycles for Hermes Agents.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It utilizes a local-first SQLite-vec and GGUF stack delivered via an MCP-native server.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It serves as a comprehensive security knowledge base for Hermes, enabling the agent to operate across 26 specialized domains with standardized defensive and offensive capabilities.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement across multiple devices and frameworks. It allows Hermes to collectively evolve its capabilities by sharing and refining learned skills within user groups or agent sessions.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. By utilizing a 109-entry word replacement table and a two-pass audit process, it ensures Hermes produces more natural, human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: This cross-platform library offers modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a marketplace or CLI. It provides Hermes with a diverse set of professional frameworks compatible with the agentskills.io specification.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on efficiency. It uses progressive disclosure of skill metadata to reduce token usage while supporting both filesystem-based and programmatic skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex XML diagrams, including UML and flowcharts, from natural language descriptions. It features an iterative review process with self-checks to ensure architectural diagrams are accurate before export.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library equips Hermes with the ability to interact with Chainlink oracle networks for tasks like cross-chain monitoring and data feed integration. It follows the agentskills.io spec to provide standardized workflows for smart contract interactions.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave together prose, diagrams, and source code into synchronized documents. It includes a tangler to extract runnable files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with shared memory across instances using markdown-based directories to track work and decisions. It includes an end-to-end pipeline for extracting training data, allowing the agent to effectively train its own replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills with support for recursive dependency resolution. It integrates commerce flows via Stripe and the TON blockchain, enabling a marketplace for professional agent capabilities.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. They allow Hermes to handle precise hex colors and structured JSON prompting for high-quality visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin watches active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It streamlines development by generating both Python scaffolds and documentation for patterns it observes during agent operations.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages state through structured missions and milestones rather than chat history. It enables Hermes to execute complex plans and generate handoff briefs for seamless session transitions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It allows Hermes to track progress in real-time and perform rollbacks if a long-running task encounters an error.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses three-layer lazy loading to connect Hermes to multiple tool servers while minimizing token consumption. It maintains persistent socket connections, allowing the agent to efficiently inspect and call a vast array of external tools.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill-forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures Hermes has verifiable audit trails by providing file-level citations for the skills it generates.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community platform for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes a trust-based feedback system and a marketplace for creators to share verified agent capabilities.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# implementation of the agentskills.io spec brings progressive disclosure and tool-based skill loading to the .NET ecosystem. It allows Hermes to integrate with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for automatic registration of common skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill enables Hermes to manage files, calendars, and contacts on Nextcloud via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup script to ensure secure communication and credential validation for personal productivity management.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages and flakes that enable instant, reproducible execution of AI coding agents. It features automated daily updates to ensure users always have access to the latest CLI-based agentic development tools.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level security layers. It offers dynamic policy presets to strictly control network and filesystem access while supporting persistent memory and over 40 built-in skills.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible, system-level deployment that supports both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skill installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI-driven experience with 100 built-in tools and support for local models via LM Studio. It functions as a portable automation engine that integrates TTS and ComfyUI without requiring Docker or complex environment setups.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for sandboxed execution. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces from a single web application. It enables hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and provides enterprise-grade MCP access for external AI integrations.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: This deployment path configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers. It is optimized for production environments requiring autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This solution deploys a Hermes agent stack via Portainer, featuring a ttyd-powered web terminal for browser-based interaction. It simplifies lifecycle management by integrating directly with Git repositories and automating image builds through GitHub Actions.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It integrates the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop sign-offs, allowing users to monitor spend and dispatch tasks to Hermes with granular control.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Designed for building autonomous swarms, this framework supports over 23 LLM providers including Hermes and local Ollama models. It features durable agent memory and a unique conversation-to-skill learning system that allows agents to acquire new capabilities through reviewed interactions.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized meta-optimizer uses an automated outer-loop search to refine harness code for Hermes Agent evaluations. By orchestrating TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, it tracks performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: This competitive arena orchestrates nine autonomous agents divided into two rival AI companies vying for GitHub stars. Utilizing Hermes Agent and Paperclip for execution, the project features a real-time dashboard to visualize agent tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains to handle complex software engineering pipelines. It enforces strict architectural integrity through mandatory quality gates and security audits for every feature, fix, or refactor.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native SDLC by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase development cycle, using automated graph-diff gates to ensure architectural quality at every stage.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project enables ACP-style delegation by routing tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It focuses on safety and isolation, employing configurable timeouts and character limits to manage delegated workflows.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding agents like Hermes and Claude Code. It provides developers with local data visualization to monitor usage trends without relying on external dashboards.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to customize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based themes, including ASCII banners and custom spinner animations. It supports partial configuration overrides, making it easy to share and iterate on community-driven visual styles.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution. It eliminates the need for OAuth or external processes while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM inference and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It provides a human-visible interface for auditing agent actions and automatically infers structured page schemas to help Hermes navigate complex web forms.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders. It enables seamless switching between global and local agents, each with its own unique set of memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter analyzes agent configurations and system prompts for structural failure modes without making any LLM calls. It uses deterministic regex scans to score prompts against HERM v1.1 standards, ensuring cost-free validation during CI/CD.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and workspace files. It handles the conversion of complex model configurations into Hermes-compatible YAML while securely transferring local credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent alongside LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and optimized routing for local GPU inference and free model usage.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This interface provides a mobile-friendly, three-panel layout that mirrors the full Hermes Agent CLI experience in a web browser. It features a unique visual context ring for real-time token monitoring alongside integrated file browsing and navigation.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard designed for multi-platform management, supporting unified configuration for eight messaging channels including Slack and WhatsApp. It combines real-time cost analytics with a web terminal and file browser for remote backend administration.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This workspace acts as a multi-agent control plane featuring a dedicated Swarm Mode and role-based dispatching. It integrates a live memory editor and a native marketplace for managing Model Context Protocol (MCP) catalogs and skills.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway for managing the Hermes stack, allowing users to start, stop, and configure individual agent profiles from a single dashboard. It includes a split-view file editor with syntax highlighting and detailed performance tracking for model usage and costs.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this native application enables direct SSH connections to Hermes environments without requiring remote helper services. It organizes agent activity through a native interface featuring Kanban boards and profile-aware session management.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React application transforms the Telegram interface into a terminal-style chat capable of spawning five concurrent background agents. It provides real-time system monitoring for hardware metrics like CPU and memory usage directly within the messaging app.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that operates without admin rights, offering a suite of over 100 tools including TTS and ComfyUI. It features a dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers at runtime and supports local model execution via LM Studio.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process with guided installation and automated dependency resolution for the Hermes Agent. It focuses on customization, providing integrated tools for editing agent personas and managing memory across 16 different messaging gateways.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A specialized monitoring dashboard that emphasizes service health, session activity, and cost tracking. It offers deep inspection capabilities through full-text search of conversation histories and a management interface for scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This self-hosted workspace utilizes real-time SSE streaming and tool approval cards to give users granular control over agent actions. It features an integrated marketplace for MCP servers and isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A lightweight, glassmorphic web interface built as a single-file React application supported by a Python proxy. It prioritizes transparency by providing live task tracking and real-time log streaming of all agent activities.
